macro_segments.title: Introduction to Rav Yosef bar Chama's Case: Seizing a Debtor's Slave for Work

micro_segments.title: Opening Discussion: Mishna on Lending and Prohibited Benefits
Rav Yosef bar Chama Seizes Slaves and Puts Them to Work
Rava Challenges His Father on the Propriety of This Practice
Rav Yosef Justifies Using Rav Nachman's Principle: Slave Value Equals Food Cost
Rava Counters: Rav Nachman Spoke Only of His Own Lazy Servant
Rav Yosef Shifts to Rav Daniel's Position: Seizing Another's Slave and Working Him is Permitted
Gemara Explains: Master Benefits from Keeping Slave Active, Not Idle
Rava Rejects This: When You Owe Money, Using Slave Looks Like Ribbit
Proof from Rav Yosef bar Minyumi: Living in Debtor's House Requires Payment Even if Normally Free
Rav Yosef Concedes: He Retracts His Position

macro_segments.title: Cases of Explicit Interest (Ribbit Ketzuza): Payment in Kind and Recovery in Court

micro_segments.title: Abayei Introduces Cases Where Borrowers Charge Interest and Later Sue for Return
Case One: Grain Payment at Higher Rate—Abayei vs. Rava on What Gets Returned
Abayei's Rule: Only the Explicitly Stipulated Amount is Returned
Rava's Counterargument: The Entire Payment Should Be Returned if Given as Interest
Abayei's New Case: Cloak Given as Interest Payment Worth More Than Interest Owed
Abayei: Only the Value of Explicit Interest is Returned, Not the Excess
Rava Disagrees: The Entire Cloak Must Be Returned to Avoid Appearance of Interest
Rava's Reasoning: Perception Issue—People Will Think It's Interest Payment
Rava's Third Case: House Rental as Interest Payment Worth More Than Interest Owed
Rav Acha miDifti Challenges: Lender Agreed to Higher Rate Willingly
Response: Lender Accepted Terms; Must Give Back Full Value of What Was Received
Conceptual Note: Protecting the Borrower, Not Penalizing the Lender

macro_segments.title: Deferred Payment in Rentals vs. Sales: The Mishna's Distinction

micro_segments.title: Mishna: You Can Increase Rental Price for Deferred Payment But Not Sale Price
Mishna Example: Renting a Yard—Discounted for Full-Year Upfront Payment
Mishna Example: Selling a Field—Forbidden to Increase Price for Deferred Payment
Gemara: Why the Difference? Rental Payment is Due Only at End of Period
Rava and Rav Yosef: For Rental, Upfront Payment is a Discount, Not Late Payment as Interest
Sale is Different: Payment is Due Immediately, So Later Payment is Interest
Rava: Rabbis Found Basis for Rental Rule in the Verse 'Like the Rental of a Year'
Tosafot Clarifies: Even if Debt Accrues Daily, It is Only Payable at Period's End

macro_segments.title: Hidden Interest (Tarsha): Pricing Without Explicit Comparison

micro_segments.title: Rav Nachman: Hidden Interest (Tarsha) is Permitted
Definition: Stating One Price Without Contrasting It to Another Price
Challenge from Mishna: But the Mishna Forbids Increasing Price for Later Payment
Rav Nachman's Response: There, the Prices Were Made Explicit; Here, Only One Price is Named
Rav Pappa: My Hidden Interest is Permissible—I Don't Need the Money
Rav Pappa's Justification: Beer Won't Spoil; I Could Sell It Later at Same Price
Challenge: But the Borrower Needs the Money—That's What Ribbit Is
Rav Sheshet's Son Refutes Rav Pappa: Look at the Buyer's Situation, Not the Seller's

macro_segments.title: Special Case: Sending Merchandise to a Distant Market

micro_segments.title: Rav Chama's Case: Buyer Takes Goods to Distant Market and Pays at Higher Price Later
The Transaction: Goods Worth One Dollar Here, Two Dollars in Distant Market
Rav Chama's Conclusion: This Tarsha is Permissible Because Buyer Receives Real Benefits
Lender's Assurance: He Insures the Product Until Sold
Buyer's Benefits: Exemption from Import Tax and First Right to Sell Due to Lender's Reputation
Tosafot's Deeper Analysis: Buyer's Favor to Lender Justifies Later Payment

macro_segments.title: Halacha Conclusions on Ribbit Ketzuza and Related Cases

micro_segments.title: Halacha: Like Rav Chama Regarding Distant Market Transaction
Halacha: Like Rabbi Eliezer—Explicit Interest (Ribbit Ketzuza) Can Be Retrieved in Court
Halacha: Like Rav Yannai—Money Can Be Returned Instead of Goods at Market Price

macro_segments.title: Hidden Interest (Tarsha): Pricing Without Explicit Comparison (continued)

micro_segments.title: Tosafot Reconciliation: Rav Nachman Permitted When No Clear Market Price; Rav Pappa's Case Has Explicit Price Difference

macro_segments.title: Mishna: Partial Payment in Land Sale and the Fruit Problem

micro_segments.title: Case One: Seller Receives Partial Payment; Sale Conditioned on Full Payment Retroactively
The Problem: If Buyer Pays Late, Who Eats the Fruit? Creates Ambiguous Ribbit Situation
Solution: Both Are Forbidden—Use Third Party to Hold Fruit Until Status Determined
Case Two: Conditional Land Pledge for Loan—Lender Seizes Property if Not Repaid
Bisos ben Zonin Example: Lending Practice with Property Forfeiture After Grace Period

macro_segments.title: Gemara Analysis: Four Scenarios for Who Eats the Fruit

micro_segments.title: Rav Huna: Seller Eats Fruit When Partial Payment Received
Sages' Opinion: Fruits Held by Third Party Until Sale Status Determined

macro_segments.title: Mishna: Partial Payment in Land Sale and the Fruit Problem (continued)

micro_segments.title: Permitted Alternative: Sale Only Completed When Full Payment Made—Seller Eats Fruit Until Then

macro_segments.title: Gemara Analysis: Four Scenarios for Who Eats the Fruit (continued)

micro_segments.title: Raba's Analysis: Four Distinct Scenarios Based on Terms of Ownership
Scenario One: Both Can Eat Fruit—Buyer Owns Proportional Share from the Start
Scenario Two: Both Forbidden to Eat—Retroactive Ownership Creates Ambiguity
Scenario Three: Seller Allowed, Buyer Forbidden—Buyer Owns Only After Full Payment
Scenario Four: Buyer Allowed, Seller Forbidden—Buyer Owns Immediately; Debt is Separate Loan
Rav Huna's Caveat: Not According to Rabbi Yehuda—He Permits One-Sided Possibility of Ribbit
Rabbi Yehuda's Logic: If One Outcome Avoids Ribbit, the Transaction is Permitted Even if Other Outcome Creates It

topical_tags.term: Zeh Neheneh v'Zeh Lo Chaser (one benefits, one doesn't lose)
Rava (son of Rav Yosef bar Chama)
Rav Nachman (on slave value and hidden interest)
Rav Pappa (hidden interest with beer case)
